target.name: Form Energy
target.founder: Mateo Jaramillo, Yet-Ming Chiang, Ted Wiley, William Woodford, Marco Ferrara

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Form Energy, a Series G climate and energy company founded by Mateo Jaramillo and co-founders, has raised over $690M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 Climate & Energy sector companies, including one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private firms such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables—only one near-peer at a comparable development stage was identified, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold required for reliable triangulation. Consequently, the valuation rests on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised ($690M+) as a $1.0B proxy baseline, a typical Series G step-up multiplier of 1.25x yields an implied valuation of $1.2B. This method reflects the capital intensity and typical investor return expectations at late-stage frontier-tech financing.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.1B to $1.4B, centered on a median of $1.2B. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent public or private comparable transactions in the narrow Climate & Energy frontier-tech segment at this stage, which limits cross-validation of the stage-progression result. Materiality hinges on Form Energy's demonstrated technical progress and path to commercial deployment, neither of which is quantified in available data.
